steps.title: Determine what the blank needs to do
steps.content: The blank fills in the subject of the relative clause "when ______ meet." The appositive that follows — "a young Indian woman from Uganda and a young African American man from Mississippi" — names two distinct people, so the blank must use the plural noun protagonists (not singular) and a possessive determiner (not a contraction) to refer back to the film.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice, then confirm the answer
steps.content:
• "it's two protagonists": It's is the contraction for "it is," not a possessive determiner — grammatically incorrect here. ✗
  • "its two protagonist's": Its is the correct possessive, but protagonist's is singular possessive; the appositive names two people, so the plural protagonists is needed. ✗
  • "it's two protagonist's": Wrong on both counts — contraction instead of possessive, and singular instead of plural. ✗
  • "its two protagonists": Its correctly refers possessively to the film, and protagonists is correctly plural to match the two characters named in the appositive. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
